stat_of_week: 19 - BaseballPro77 set a new single-week team HR record with 19 home runs, led by Mike Trout's absurd 6-HR, 11-RBI, .472 OBP demolition of the league
thriller_note: These two evenly matched squads deadlocked across nine categories with neither able to pull away, leaving both teams with a frustrating tie that does little to separate them in what's shaping up to be a bottom-half battle for playoff positioning.
key_storyline: BaseballPro77 put on the most dominant offensive performance of the young season, smashing a league-record 19 home runs and 39 RBI while posting a 2.33 ERA on the mound for a 7-2 category beatdown of the Wisconsin Rapids Reapers. Mike Trout was the engine — his 6 HR, 11 RBI, and .472 OBP line looked less like a fantasy week and more like a video game cheat code. With both the HR and RBI single-week records now belonging to BaseballPro77, this team is announcing itself as the most dangerous roster in the league heading into the back half of the first month.
lucky_reason: Despite posting bottom-of-the-league marks in HR (4), strikeouts (45), and hits, the Benchwarmers managed a 1.4 actual win contribution against an expected 0.19 — the largest positive luck delta in the league this week — largely because they caught General Hospital in a favorable spot despite being outgunned in almost every meaningful category.
unlucky_reason: The Twins posted a genuinely strong week — 14 HR, 37 runs, 8 SB, and a José Ramírez line that was among the best in the league — but their expected win total of 1.81 far outpaced their actual contribution of 1.2, reflecting the worst luck delta (-0.61) in the league and masking a roster that should be winning more than it is.
records_broken: BaseballPro77 broke two single-week team records: most HR in a week with 19 (previous: implied prior high) and most RBI in a week with 39 (previous mark: 39 set this same week, confirming as new benchmark per season record data).
power_rankings: 1. BaseballPro77 | League-record 19 HR and 39 RBI in a single week; Trout is playing like an MVP and the pitching is holding up — this is the team to beat | up
2. General Hospital | League-best 8 category wins this week, 77.1 IP, 6 QS, and a 2.68 ERA; Will Warren and a deep rotation make this team quietly terrifying | same
3. Sugar Land Skeeters | Took a loss but remain the most consistent top-tier performer across all four weeks; Tatis, Happ, and Weathers give them multi-category upside every night | same
4. Athletic Park Dragons | Judge's 6-HR week headlines a 5-2 win; Cristopher Sánchez and Bailey Ober both looked sharp and this pitching staff is deepening | same
5. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | League-leading 8 QS and a 2.49 ERA remain the calling card; Paul Skenes' 0.90 ERA and Imanaga bouncing back make the rotation elite | same
6. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Ramirez's .500 OBP and 4-HR week was jaw-dropping; bad luck continues to suppress the record but the talent is undeniable | up
7. Hiroshima Radiation | McLean and Eovaldi both posted 1.50 ERAs and 5 combined QS — the rotation is for real, but the offense remains the Achilles heel | down
8. North Correa | Chris Sale's 0.69 ERA and 16 K effort was elite; Ohtani and Hoerner give them a high floor even if the pitching outside Sale is inconsistent | same
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Buxton led a solid offensive week and the team won 5 categories; the wave of waiver additions shows active management trying to patch real holes | same
10. Riverside Roughnecks | Tied when they likely should have won; Langeliers, Kurtz, and Misiorowski are legitimate pieces but this team needs more consistency across the lineup | same
11. Canberra Wombats | A 4-4 tie is better than a loss but Gunnar Henderson's .189 OBP and a thin roster ceiling remain serious concerns heading into a tough stretch | up
12. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Got torched 7-2 by the hottest team in the league; Ben Rice remains a genuine bright spot but 33 hits and a .257 OBP are roster-wide problems | down
13. Buttville Skidmarks | Reynolds' .500 OBP was completely wasted again as the pitching posted a 4.87 ERA; this team loses in the same way every week | same

14. Benchwarmers | Michael King's 19 K and 1.59 ERA are genuinely great but the rest of this roster — 4 HR, 45 K, bottom-five in nearly every offensive category — is not viable without significant additions | same
waiver_highlights: Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A | Added Paul Skenes, Ildemaro Vargas, Scott Barlow, Dustin May, Masyn Winn in a single wave | Skenes delivered immediately with a 0.90 ERA and 11 K, Vargas contributed a .600 OBP off the bench — this was the most impactful waiver week in the league

General Hospital | B | Added Kumar Rocker (SP); dropped Jack Leiter, Janson Junk, Cristian Javier | Smart roster cleanup replacing three underperformers with one high-upside arm; reflects a team managing depth intelligently while already winning

Albuquerque Bombers | B | Added Keaton Winn, Noelvi Marte, Braxton Garrett, Josh Jung; dropped three roster fillers | Four adds in one week signals aggressive roster building; quality is uncertain but the volume and intent earns credit

North Correa | C | Added Austin Wells and Brayan Rocchio; dropped J.T. Realmuto and Brooks Lee | Upgrading catcher with Wells makes sense but losing Realmuto feels like selling low; Rocchio is a low-upside flier

Riverside Roughnecks | C | Added Victor Vodnik (RP); dropped Mark Vientos (1B/3B) | Losing Vientos's positional flexibility and power upside for a fringe reliever is a questionable value exchange for a team that needs more bats
